evidence_sentence: Loss-of-function mutant of MOC1 exhibits a monoculm phenotype without tillers.
Overexpression of MOC1 leads to the formation of higher-order tillers such as tertiary, quaternary, and quinary.
moc3 mutant cannot form normal axillary meristems; severely reduced tillers.
Loss of MOC2 causes a significant reduction in tiller number.
lax1 single mutant shows reduced axillary bud formation.
lax2 single mutant: sparse panicle; reduced secondary branches and spikelets; fewer tillers.
notes: OsLAX2 not represented as a separate node; LAX1 node is LAX1-specific

evidence_sentence: lax1 lax2 double mutant: severe reduction in secondary branches and spikelets; dramatic tiller loss; 'All SBs and spikelets except terminal spikelets were absent'.
notes: lax1 lax2 double — LAX2 absent; only LAX1 encoded

evidence_sentence: lax2 moc1 double mutant: complete loss of all branching except primary branches; monoculm.
notes: lax2 moc1 — LAX2 not in network; only MOC1 encoded

evidence_sentence: tab1-1 produces no tillers even in 2-month-old shoots.
In fon2 mutant sizes of the SAM and inflorescence meristem were increased; axillary meristems increased.
In FON2 overexpressor, axillary meristem formation is inhibited.
tab1 fon2 double mutant produces a few tillers (partial rescue of tab1 tillerless phenotype).
notes: tab1 fon2 double — TAB1 not in network; only FON2 encoded

evidence_sentence: d3 mutant is tillering dwarf with excessive tillers.
notes: d3 KO — SL F-box lost, high tillers

evidence_sentence: d14 mutant shows accelerated outgrowth of tillers; SL-insensitive.
notes: d14 KO — SL receptor lost, high tillers

evidence_sentence: d53 dominant mutant has total tiller number about three times that of wild type.
notes: d53-D dominant GOF — stabilised D53 (Trap-5 encoding)

evidence_sentence: pActin::D53-GFP transgenic plants showed moderate increase in tillering.
d10 SL-biosynthesis mutant shows high-tillering dwarf phenotype.
d17/htd1 SL-biosynthesis mutant shows high-tillering dwarf phenotype.
d27 final tiller number is three times that of the wild type.
d88/d14L weak SL-insensitive phenotype with moderately increased tillers.
notes: D88 dwarf locus not represented in network

evidence_sentence: Os900 max1 mutant shows increased tillers.
fc1 mutant (OsTB1 loss-of-function) causes increase in the number of tillers; NIL-fc1 produces 143% more panicles.
Transgenic rice plants overexpressing OsTB1 exhibited markedly reduced lateral branching.
ipa1-1D: miR156 binding site mutation, IPA1 accumulation, reduced tiller number.
notes: ipa1-1D — miR156 binding-site GOF, stabilised IPA1 (Trap: GOF=2.0, NOT KO)

evidence_sentence: ipa1-2D novel allele generates large panicles and moderate panicle number, with reduced tillers.
notes: ipa1-2D — miR156 binding-site GOF, stabilised IPA1

evidence_sentence: ipa1-10 loss-of-function: more tillers, smaller panicles.
miR156 OE degrades OsSPL14 mRNA, resulting in increased tiller number.
notes: miR156 OE degrades IPA1 → high tillers

evidence_sentence: miR529a OE increases tiller number by targeting OsSPL14.
notes: miR529 mapped to miR156 node (shares SPL target site, same tillering phenotype)

evidence_sentence: Gn1a reduced-function allele: more tillers and more grains per panicle due to CK accumulation.
notes: Gn1a is OsCKX2 reduced-function allele → CKX2 gm=0.0

evidence_sentence: OsCKX2-RNAi: 27-81% increased panicle/tiller number vs wild-type.
OsCKX2-OE: reduced tillers (gene-dosage dependent).
osckx4 increases tillers via CK accumulation.
notes: OsCKX4 not separately in network (CKX2, CKX9 only)

evidence_sentence: osckx9 mutant: increased tillers, decreased plant height and panicle size.
OsCKX9-OX: increased tillers, decreased height and panicle branches (paradox â€” CK depletion in node but altered SL response).
osckx11 increases tiller number and alters source-sink relationship.
OsIPT OE increases cytokinin levels; expected to promote tillering.
notes: OsIPT4 single OE → IPT family node (CK biosynthesis gene family)

evidence_sentence: osipt4/7 loss reduces CK biosynthesis and expected reduced tiller outgrowth.
notes: OsIPT4/OsIPT7 double KO → IPT composite collapsed, gm=0.0

evidence_sentence: OsAHP-RNAi plants exhibited phenotypes expected for a deficiency in cytokinin signaling, including dwarfism and reduced tiller numbers.
ohk4 loss-of-function: reduced panicle, decreased higher-order tillers.
ngr5 loss-of-function mutant failed to induce tiller outgrowth in response to nitrogen.
NGR5-OE/high-expression allele: increased tiller number and yield.
Accessions with 29-bp insertion in OsTCP19 promoter (loss of expression) show high tillering response to nitrogen.
OsTCP19-OE: reduced tillers via DLT repression.
dlt mutant: dwarf and low tillering phenotype.
OsLBD37-OE: represses OsTCP19 and increases tillers.
Indica NRT1.1B allele in japonica background increases tiller number and NUE.
notes: NRT1.1B indica allele hyperactive — encoded as NRT1_1B gm=2.0

evidence_sentence: osnlp4 mutant: significantly decreased tiller number and plant height.
notes: OsNLP4 transcription factor not in network

evidence_sentence: tad1 mutant showed an increased tiller number.
notes: tad1 KO — MOC1 stabilised, more tillers

evidence_sentence: TAD1-overexpressing transgenic plants showed a reduced tiller number.
te mutant displays drastically increased tiller number (~3-fold).
TE-OE reduced tiller number.
te moc1-5 double: reduced tillers like moc1-5 alone; MOC1 epistatic over TE.
slr1 recessive mutant exhibited fewer tillers and taller stature.
notes: slr1 LOF — DELLA lost, constitutive GA response

evidence_sentence: SLR1-OE: tiller number was increased.
slr1 moc1 double: low tillers like moc1 alone (moc1 epistatic).
sd1 (GA20ox2 null) produces more tillers and shorter plants.
GA20ox1-OE line: fewer tillers and taller stature.
ipi1-1 CRISPR mutant: reduced IPA1 in shoot apex causing increased tillers.
Ub:IPI1-OE: increased IPA1 accumulation in shoot apex, reduced tillers.
shi1 mutant: dramatically reduced tiller number.
OsSHI1-OE: reduced plant height, decreased panicle, ectopic tillers at upper internodes but reduced normal tillers.
notes: OsSHI1 OE — IPA1 suppressed (paper reports decreased)

evidence_sentence: osmads57-1 activation-tagged: 28 tillers vs WT 10.
OsMADS57-antisense: reduced tillers.
OsMIR444a-OE lines: suppresses OsMADS57 expression, fewer tillers.
notes: miR444a OE → cleaves MADS57 → derepresses D14

evidence_sentence: oscca1 mutant shows more tillers and delayed flowering.
OsCCA1-OE: fewer tillers than transgenic control.
OsPRR1-OE: reduced tiller numbers.
Knockdown of OsPIN1a decreases crown roots and increases tiller number.
notes: OsPIN1a separate paralog from PIN1B; not in network

evidence_sentence: OsPIN1b CRISPR mutant: increased tiller numbers, altered root gravitropism.
OsPIN2-OE leads to increased tiller numbers, tiller angle and shorter plant.
ospin9 knockout: decreased tillering.
OsPIN9-OE: increased tillering.
OsTIR1/OsAFB2 double mutants show severely compromised plant development including tillering defects.
notes: OsTIR1 + OsAFB2 double — only TIR1 in network; AFB2 absent

evidence_sentence: japonica OsTB2-3bp+ allele: promotes axillary bud elongation, increased tillers.
notes: OsTB2 paralog of TB1 not separately encoded

evidence_sentence: reg1 allele of DST: reduced OsCKX2 expression, elevated CK, enhanced grain number and moderately increased tillers (Gn1a-independent +20% yield).
notes: DST reg1 allele is a semi-dominant LOF that reduces DST binding to OsCKX2 promoter; re-encoded from gm=2.0 to gm=0.5. Evidence: Li 2013 PNAS.

evidence_sentence: DST-OE: activates OsCKX2, reduced CK, reduced panicle branches and grain number; reduces tillers.
notes: DST OE (paper reports decreased tillers via CKX2 induction)

evidence_sentence: WT + 10uM GR24 completely inhibited the outgrowth of third leaf tiller buds.
d27 + 1.0 uM GR24 fully rescues tillering dwarf phenotype.
d10-2 + 1 uM GR24: sufficient to inhibit the outgrowth of first and second leaf buds; SL-biosynthesis-mutant responds.
d17 SL-biosynthesis mutant + GR24: phenotype rescued; SL-responsive.
d3-2 + 10uM GR24: tillers not suppressed; SL-insensitive.
notes: d3 + GR24 Trap-5: F-box lost, no D53 degradation, unchanged

evidence_sentence: d14 + GR24 treatment: axillary buds still grow (SL-insensitive).
notes: d14 + GR24 Trap-5: receptor lost, unchanged

evidence_sentence: d53 is resistant to exogenous application of GR24.
notes: d53-D + GR24 Trap-5: stabilised D53 cannot be degraded, unchanged

evidence_sentence: fc1 + 10uM GR24: third and fourth leaf tiller buds were not affected (SL-insensitive via FC1 requirement).
notes: fc1 (=ostb1) + GR24 — downstream of D53, GR24 cannot suppress without TB1

evidence_sentence: BAP (cytokinin) treatment suppresses FC1 expression and promotes tiller bud outgrowth.
Apical IAA application suppresses bud outgrowth via BRC1/FC1 induction.
NAA treatment rescued OsPIN1 RNAi phenotype and suppressed WT tillering.
NPA (auxin transport inhibitor, similar to TIBA) treatment largely rescued tiller number; blocks polar auxin transport.
notes: TIBA = auxin transport inhibitor → PIN1B knockdown analog

evidence_sentence: GA (10^-5 M) 1 month treatment: decreased tiller number.
PBZ (paclobutrazol, GA inhibitor) significantly increased tiller number and decreased plant height.
notes: PBZ paclobutrazol = GA biosynthesis inhibitor → GA20OX2 KD

evidence_sentence: High nitrogen fertilization causes downregulation of OsTB1 via NGR5-PRC2 H3K27me3 and promotes tiller formation.
Low nitrogen: reduced tillers, NGR5 not accumulated.
notes: Low N encoded as Nitrogen gm=0.5 (graded source)

evidence_sentence: ngr5 + high N: no tiller response to nitrogen (N-insensitive) compared to ngr5 alone.
notes: ngr5 + high N unchanged (N-insensitive due to NGR5 loss)

evidence_sentence: Low P conditions elevate SL biosynthesis genes D10/D17 and reduce tiller bud outgrowth.
notes: Low phosphate encoded as Phosphate gm=0.5

evidence_sentence: ipa1-1D significantly suppressed the high tillering phenotype of d53.
ipa1-1D could repress the high-tillering phenotype of d27.
35S-FC1/d3-2 lines showed partial rescue of d3 defects (plant height, tiller number, stem thickness).
notes: FC1-OE in d3 background — TB1 OE downstream, partial rescue

evidence_sentence: D53-RNAi in d3 background obviously reduced the tiller number.
notes: D53-RNAi in d3 background — rescues d3 high-tillering

evidence_sentence: D53-RNAi in d14 background: tiller number restored to nearly wild type levels.
notes: D53-RNAi in d14 background — rescues d14 high-tillering

evidence_sentence: d14 d53 double mutant showed dwarf tillering phenotype resembling d14 and d53 parental plants (no additive effect, epistatic).
notes: d14 d53-D double — both high-tillering, epistatic

evidence_sentence: d3 d53 double: dwarf tillering phenotype resembling d3; d3 phenotype epistatic over d53.
d27d10 double mutant showed similar tiller number and plant height to d10 (d10 epistatic).
notes: d27 d10 double — SL biosynthesis blocked

evidence_sentence: lax1 moc1 double mutant shows enhanced suppression of axillary meristem formation.
Exogenous sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice.
Sucrose effect on tillering partially depends on D53; d53 does not further increase with sucrose.
notes: sucrose on d53-D — D53 already maximal, no further change

evidence_sentence: Panicle removal (decapitation, DeP): increased sugar content, reduced OsCCA1 expression, threefold tiller increase.
Leaf removal (defoliation, DeF): decreased sugars, elevated OsCCA1, reduced tillers.
notes: Defoliation → reduced photosynthate → Sucrose gm=0.5 analog

evidence_sentence: gt1 mutant: increased tiller bud outgrowth.
~110 kb deletion eliminating PROG1 cluster increased tiller number (domestication).
notes: PROG1 domestication locus not in network

evidence_sentence: tig1 allele: pleiotropic effects including altered tiller number.
notes: TIG1 (TILLER INCLINED GROWTH) not in network

evidence_sentence: Knocking out OsAAP3 can promote growth of buds and effective tillers.
notes: OsAAP3 amino-acid permease not in network

evidence_sentence: OsAAP5-OE: altered amino acid levels affect CK biosynthesis; reduced tillers.
gs1;2 mutant: reduced tiller bud growth due to reduced CK production.
notes: GS1.2 glutamine synthetase not in network

evidence_sentence: osbzr1 loss: failure to recruit D53 to OsTB1 promoter; increased tiller outgrowth.
Exogenous BR stabilizes OsBZR1-D53 complex, representing OsTB1 and reducing tiller outgrowth.
Blocking of OsAAP7 promoted tillering and yield by determining basic and neutral amino acids accumulation.
osvil2 shows defective axillary bud outgrowth, reduced tillers.
Natural variation in Tn1a promoter increases rice tillering via TIF1 interaction.
notes: TN1 (Tn1a promoter variant) not in network

evidence_sentence: dep1 mutant: reduced panicle length, reduced grain number, altered tiller number.
notes: DEP1 panicle architecture gene not in network

evidence_sentence: asn1 mutant reduces asparagine concentration and inhibits tiller outgrowth.
notes: ASN1 asparagine synthetase not in network

evidence_sentence: Overexpression of OsTB1 partially suppressed the tiller-overproducing phenotype of dwarf3 (d3).
notes: OsTB1-OE in d3 background — same as T097 duplicate entry

evidence_sentence: d10 d3 double: high-tillering dwarf like each single mutant (parallel pathways converge).
notes: d10 d3 double — SL biosynthesis + signalling both lost

evidence_sentence: d14 d53 double: resembling d14 and d53 parental plants.
notes: d14 d53-D double — duplicate of T100 from second source

evidence_sentence: Kinetin (cytokinin) treatment suppresses FC1 expression and promotes bud outgrowth.
BAP treatment clearly suppressed FC1 expression in a concentration-dependent manner and promoted bud outgrowth.
NPF7.7-OE increases effective tiller number by enhancing N uptake.
notes: NPF7.7 nitrate transporter not in network

evidence_sentence: NPF7.1-OE promotes rice axillary buds outgrowth.
npf7.4 knockout promotes rice axillary buds outgrowth.
SL biosynthesis mutant combinations give similar high-tiller phenotype (d10 d14 etc).
fon1 mutant forms axillary buds normally but fails to elongate tiller buds.
osarf12 osarf17 osarf25 triple mutant: altered tiller angle and increased tillers.
notes: OsARF12/17/25 triple — ARF genes absent; mapped to TIR1 KO (auxin signalling loss, same phenotypic direction)

evidence_sentence: OsIAA stabilized variants block auxin signaling, partially derepress bud outgrowth.
notes: Stabilised OsIAA blocks ARF response → analog of TIR1 KO

evidence_sentence: OsYUCCA mutants: reduced auxin synthesis affects crown roots and altered tiller dynamics.
notes: OsYUC family KO → YUCCA composite node

evidence_sentence: OsTAR2 loss reduces IAA biosynthesis, affecting architecture and tiller number.
oslax1/2/3 triple mutant: strongly reduced axillary meristem formation.
notes: oslax1 oslax2 oslax3 triple — only LAX1 in network

evidence_sentence: d53 ipa1-1D double: IPA1 accumulation suppresses d53 high tillering.
notes: ipa1-1D d53-D double — same as T095 (second source)

evidence_sentence: Ub:IPI1 in ipa1-1D background: reverted thick culm and panicle size phenotypes of ipa1-1D (IPI1 dominant).
notes: Ub:IPI1 in ipa1-1D — IPI1 OE degrades IPA1 despite GOF

evidence_sentence: OsSHI1-OE/35S:IPA1-Flag double OE: recovered tiller development relative to 35S:IPA1 alone (OsSHI1 inhibits IPA1).
With or without BAP treatment, levels of FC1 mRNA in d3 and d10 were not significantly different; BAP acts independently of SL but d3 background already high-tillering.
notes: CK treatment in d3 — additive CK

evidence_sentence: T6P signaling (via TPS overexpression or sucrose) promotes bud outgrowth.
Exogenous orobanchol (natural rice SL) inhibits tiller outgrowth similar to GR24.
rac-GR24 synthetic SL: inhibits axillary bud outgrowth in WT.
Reducing D53 expression in d53 background drastically reduced the tiller number.
Wild-type Nipponbare produces characteristic 10-15 tillers at heading stage (baseline).
moc1 d10 double mutant: monoculm (moc1 epistatic over high-tillering d10).
osckx9 d27 double mutant: intermediate phenotype between singles.
Upon SL treatment, tiller number is unchanged in ipa1-10 (loss-of-function) and d14 ipa1-10; IPA1 required for SL-mediated tiller inhibition.
notes: IPA1-OE in d14 — IPA1 downstream, rescues via TB1/D53 feedback

evidence_sentence: oscca1 d14 double: genetic data confirm D14 acts downstream; phenotype like d14.
oscca1 ostb1 double: phenotype resembles ostb1 single mutant (ostb1 epistatic).
shi1 ipa1 double: phenotype resembles ipa1 single (ipa1 downstream of shi1).
shi1 dep1 double: phenotype resembles dep1 single (dep1 downstream).
notes: shi1 dep1 — DEP1 absent; only SHI1 encoded

evidence_sentence: ohk4 ipa1 double: phenotype similar to ipa1; IPA1 is downstream of OsHK4 in panicle feedback.
OsRR21-OE in ohk4 background: partial rescue of panicle and tiller defects.
NGR5-OE + GA treatment: GA-induced NGR5 degradation partially counteracts OE effect.
NGR5-high-expression allele + high nitrogen: maximal tiller number and yield (synergy).
Under low N, d53 mutant still shows more tillers than WT; d53 partially insensitive to N-status.
notes: Low N + d53-D — d53 still high-tillering even under N-limit

evidence_sentence: Under N-deficient conditions, tiller number reduced in both WT and d3 and d10; N effect partially SL-independent.
notes: Low N + d3 — low-N suppresses d3 high-tillering

evidence_sentence: ngr5 + GR24: SL signaling intact but N response absent; tillers reduced similar to GR24 alone (N-insensitive but SL-sensitive).
notes: ngr5 + GR24 — SL signalling intact, tillers reduced

evidence_sentence: Shading reduces photosynthesis and sucrose, reducing tillers via increased OsCCA1.
Drought stress increases ABA and decreases tillers via NCED1 induction.
bzr1 d53 double: high-tillering like d53 (d53 epistatic for tiller count).
ngr5 + PBZ: GA blockade stabilizes SLR1 but no NGR5 to convert signal; moderate tiller increase via SLR1-MOC1 axis.
notes: ngr5 + PBZ — GA-biosynthesis blockade stabilises SLR1, SLR1-MOC1 axis still active

evidence_sentence: SLR1-OE resistant to GA degradation: retains high tiller phenotype with GA treatment.
notes: slr1-d SLR1-OE + GA — DELLA cannot be degraded, high tillers retained

evidence_sentence: Ammonium supply promotes tillers via OsPIN9 induction and CK production.
notes: GS+NH4+ treatment — Ammonium exogenous=1.0 (GS enzyme absent)

evidence_sentence: RCN127 F-box protein enhances rice tillering by mediating degradation of OsTB1 and OsTCP19.
osstp15 knockout: increased tiller number due to higher sugar content in shoot base.
notes: OsSTP15 sugar transporter not in network

evidence_sentence: miR396 OE targets OsGRF TFs affecting panicle and tillers indirectly.
notes: miR396 not in network (different family from miR156/miR444)

evidence_sentence: Sucrose antagonizes GR24 inhibition of tillering by stabilizing D53.
notes: GR24 + sucrose combined — sucrose dominates, increased tillers

evidence_sentence: Decapitation + GR24: GR24 partially blocks tiller outgrowth induced by decapitation (SL acts downstream of sugar signal).
notes: Decapitation + GR24 — GR24 suppresses decapitation release

evidence_sentence: d14 d53 + GR24: GR24 does not reduce tillers (SL signaling blocked at receptor + repressor levels).
notes: d14 d53-D + GR24 — Trap 5, SL pathway blocked at both levels

evidence_sentence: SLR1-OE in tad1 background: tiller number additively elevated (both stabilize MOC1).
OsHK6 is cytokinin receptor; loss slightly reduces CK signaling and tillers.
notes: OsHK6 paralog of HK4 not separately encoded

evidence_sentence: OsRR type-A OE: negative feedback on CK signaling reduces tillers.
notes: OsRR type-A OE negative feedback on CK

evidence_sentence: OsWOX4 plays role in maintaining axillary meristem after establishment; loss reduces tiller maintenance.
notes: OsWOX4 meristem gene not in network

evidence_sentence: MP3 allele of OsTB1/FC1: moderately increased tiller/panicle number without compromising spikelet.
asn1 + high N: failed to increase tillers normally seen with high N (Asn essential for N signaling to buds).
notes: ASN1 + N — ASN1 absent; cannot encode mutant effect

evidence_sentence: osvil2 ostb1 double: complex phenotype, reduced overall tillers.
Cold stress: OsMADS57+OsTB1 switch to defense/cold mode, reducing D14 expression and tiller outgrowth.
ACC (ethylene precursor) treatment slightly reduces tiller outgrowth in rice.
OsIPT7-OE increases cytokinin levels and promotes tillering.
notes: OsIPT7 single OE → IPT family node

evidence_sentence: Takanari wild-type at 12th leaf: 10 tillers (4 primary, 6 secondary) baseline reference.
notes: WT tillers at heading — negative control

evidence_sentence: NIL-MP3 at 12th leaf: 13 tillers (5 primary, 8 secondary) +28% panicles vs Takanari.
NIL-fc1 at 12th leaf: 31 tillers (7 primary, 17 secondary, 7 tertiary) +143% panicles vs Takanari; thinner lighter tillers cause lodging.
notes: NIL-fc1 — fc1 = OsTB1 LOF in NIL

evidence_sentence: NIL-MP3 in P-deficient soil still tended to produce more tillers than Takanari.
MP3 further enhances above-ground biomass by 3.5% at elevated CO2 (579 umol/mol).
notes: Elevated CO2 + NIL-MP3 — MP3 absent, CO2 node absent

evidence_sentence: OsYUCCA1-OE elevates auxin, reduces tillers via enhanced apical dominance.
Tiller number of double mutant osvil2 ostb1 was similar to that of ostb1, suggesting that osvil2 is epistatic to ostb1.
notes: osvil2 ostb1 double — duplicate of T177

evidence_sentence: OsGATA8-L haplotype shows reduced productive tiller proportion via failure to repress OsTCP19.
The OsGATA8-H haplotype is identified as a high-NUE haplotype with enhanced nitrogen uptake and a higher proportion of productive tillers.
CRISPR/Cas9 mutants of Os1900 (and Os5100) show altered SL biosynthesis and tillering responses.
Os1900 promoter mutations alone can increase tiller numbers and grain yields even under minor-fertilizer conditions.
notes: Os1900 promoter mutation — partial LOF, gm=0.5

evidence_sentence: Deficiency of DTN1 results in reduced photosynthetic rate and decreased contents of sucrose; reduced tiller number in dtn1 mutant.
Reduced tiller number in dtn1 mutant could be partially rescued by exogenous sucrose treatment.
notes: dtn1 + sucrose partial rescue via Sucrose supply

evidence_sentence: Overexpression of NGR5 in the dtn1-2 could partially rescue the reduced tiller number.
notes: NGR5-OE in dtn1 — partial rescue via NGR5 pathway

evidence_sentence: Researchers identified a high-tillering mutant tillering20 (t20), which could be restored to the wild type by treatment with the strigolactone analog rac-GR24.
notes: t20 KO — SL biosynthesis cofactor lost

evidence_sentence: t20 high tillering could be restored to the wild type by treatment with the strigolactone analog rac-GR24.
ABA treatment significantly reduced the number of basal tillers in both t20 and wild-type plants.
ABA treatment significantly reduced the number of basal tillers in both t20 and wild-type plants.
Overexpression of OsDRM1 in transgenic plants delayed axillary bud outgrowth by suppressing cell division within the buds.
notes: OsDRM1 dormancy gene not in network

evidence_sentence: The plant heights of ohk3 ohk4 and ohk4 ohk5 double mutants were slightly but significantly reduced, while the tiller numbers varied.
notes: oshk3 oshk4 double — HK3 absent; only HK4 encoded

evidence_sentence: Excessive nitrogen promotes the formation of nonproductive tillers; OsGATA8-L (loss) accessions show reduced productive tillering even under high N.
notes: OsGATA8 LOF + high N — still reduced productive tillers

evidence_sentence: Os1900 mutants escape N-induced SL biosynthesis and remain high-tillering even under high fertilizer.
notes: Os1900 KO + high N — escapes N-induced SL biosynthesis
